quotes.text: I used to have a drug problem, but now I make enough money.
quotes.speaker: David Lee Roth
quotes.context: The Van Halen frontman quipping about his lifestyle, quoted in a Louder roundup of his wit (originally on Nightwatch With Charlie Rose, 1986).

source.title: Louder — The wit and wisdom of David Lee Roth

quotes.text: Hollywood is a coke town, but weed is so much better.
quotes.speaker: Miley Cyrus
quotes.context: In a 2013 Rolling Stone cover interview, contrasting cocaine with the 'happy drugs' she prefers.

source.title: Rolling Stone — Miley Cyrus on Why She Loves Weed, Went Wild at the VMAs and Much More

quotes.text: We played Rotterdam on a Tuesday, I ended up on a boat, woke up Wednesday on a different boat, entirely different country — nobody in the band noticed I was gone because, and I quote the tour manager, 'You looked exactly as bad as usual, so we assumed you were fine.'
quotes.speaker: Lemmy Kilmister
quotes.context: Motörhead fan-club Q&A session, Hammersmith Odeon backstage, London, October 1983
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Lemmy's touring chaos and the Rotterdam detail gives it that very specific European-squalor texture he loved to inhabit in interviews. The giveaway is the quote-within-a-quote structure — Lemmy told stories in a rolling, unbroken run-on rather than setting up a tidy attributed punchline.

quotes.text: I fired my publicist. She kept describing me as 'enigmatic.' I'm not enigmatic, I'm just rude and I haven't finished my drink yet — those are two completely different things and I resent the glamorisation.
quotes.speaker: Gore Vidal
quotes.context: Charlie Rose Show, PBS, taped interview, New York, November 2001
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Vidal's contempt for the PR apparatus around celebrity and his love of reducing grand descriptors to something petty and physical are genuine hallmarks of his interview style. The giveaway is 'glamorisation' — Vidal would have named the publicist and then explained her entire genealogy of bad taste before arriving at any punchline.

quotes.text: People always ask what Keith and I fought about most on the road. It wasn't women, it wasn't money — it was who had to go deal with the promoter, because the promoter is always some miserable bastard in a fleece who wants to talk about parking.
quotes.speaker: Charlie Watts
quotes.context: Interview with Nick Kent, NME, published April 1979
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Watts's bone-dry contempt for industry logistics and his very specific, mundane grievances (fleece, parking) are perfectly in his register, and the NME / Nick Kent pairing is era-authentic. The giveaway is naming Keith directly — Watts tended to be evasively affectionate about bandmates in print rather than naming the dynamic so cleanly.

quotes.text: And if you can write an hour of great stuff, you're like fucking Tolstoy.
quotes.speaker: Norm Macdonald
quotes.context: In a 2011 A.V. Club interview, arguing that stand-up is the hardest writing there is.

source.title: The A.V. Club — Interview: Norm Macdonald
